title: How Do You Pronounce
wikitext:
[]: How Do You Pronounce....? Post by xtr00kvltcorex » Thu Jul 16, 2009 12:37 pm i has a dumb question. how do you pronounce "Tabris"? For some reason i always pronounce it "tah-bree" or is the "s" pronounced.

[]: Post by schismatics » Thu Jul 16, 2009 1:36 pm I always thought it was Ta-bree.

[]: Post by Rock-Slash » Thu Jul 16, 2009 1:38 pm The "S" is pronounced.

[]: Post by Sammaeloo » Thu Jul 16, 2009 1:43 pm I have always called it Tah-bris, with the "a" sounding like the "a" in ball or the "o" in rock. I'm not sure if the "a" is supposed to sound like the "a" in cat or flat.

[]: Post by Squirrel Ninja » Thu Jul 16, 2009 2:00 pm I've assumed it was Tab[like the key]-Ris[rhymes with kiss].

[]: Post by xtr00kvltcorex » Thu Jul 16, 2009 3:26 pm schismatics wrote: I always thought it was Ta-bree.

[]: Post by Marduk » Thu Jul 16, 2009 3:42 pm Hmm... I've always pronounced it as "Tab-reez" =|

[]: Post by NickFury90 » Thu Jul 16, 2009 4:17 pm Off-topic, but any reason for that particular quote from the greatest movie ever made, xtr00kvltcorex?

[]: Post by VoidEater » Thu Jul 16, 2009 4:55 pm As a Hebrew name: Anglocized: Ta (a as in Cat) bris (i as in which). Yiddish: Ta (a as in cat) brees (ee as in breeze, soft s as in sentimental). First syllable is emphasized: Ta' bris. Some speakers seem to add into the soft s a slight somewhat whistling z, but this is not predominant. I don't know what a Japanese speaker would turn this into, or how the Japanese writers intended the word to be pronounced. This is how it would be pronounced as a Hebrew personal pronoun.

[]: Post by xtr00kvltcorex » Fri Jul 17, 2009 5:00 am NickFury90 wrote: Off-topic, but any reason for that particular quote from the greatest movie ever made, xtr00kvltcorex?

[]: Post by Reichu » Sun Jul 19, 2009 6:48 pm VoidEater wrote: I don't know what a Japanese speaker would turn this into

[]: Post by GVman » Sun Jul 19, 2009 10:59 pm VoidEater wrote: As a Hebrew name: I don't know what a Japanese speaker would turn this into, or how the Japanese writers intended the word to be pronounced. This is how it would be pronounced as a Hebrew personal pronoun.

[]: Post by BrikHaus » Mon Jul 20, 2009 1:19 am GVman wrote: Taburisu?

[]: Post by Shinji-kun » Tue Jul 28, 2009 10:13 am I'd say it's Tabrisu [Tah-boo-ree-sue] for you americans.

[]: Post by Fazmotron » Tue Jul 28, 2009 11:14 am The su would be shortened to ssssssssssssssssssssssssssssssssssssssss.

[]: Post by Shinji-kun » Tue Jul 28, 2009 11:35 am Fazmotron wrote: The su would be shortened to ssssssssssssssssssssssssssssssssssssssss.

[]: Post by Ornette » Tue Jul 28, 2009 1:32 pm I think this thread has served its purpose
